steps.title: Identify the parallel pair
steps.content: The sentence describes two hands as a parallel pair: "one hand [blank] and the other [description]." Both parts must follow the same grammatical form.

steps.title: Match the parallel form
steps.content:
The blank is part of an absolute phrase: "one hand [description] and the other [description], the hands imply..." Both halves must be grammatically parallel.
  • Choice A — is bare and the other is: "one hand is bare and the other is covered in Persian script, the hands imply" — the finite verb "is" creates a full clause, making the comma before "the hands" a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — bare, the other is: "one hand bare, the other is covered in Persian script, the hands imply" — "the other is covered" has a finite verb; the comma after it before "the hands" is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — bare and the other: "one hand bare and the other covered in Persian script, the hands imply" — both halves are adjective-only; the whole phrase is a parallel absolute that modifies "the hands." ✓
  • Choice D — is bare, the other: "one hand is bare, the other covered in Persian script, the hands imply" — "one hand is bare" is a finite clause separated by a comma from "the other covered," creating a comma splice before a non-parallel fragment. ✗
That matches Choice C — bare and the other.
